violations.text: Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

The dipper well faucet at the ice cream scoop dipper well was hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. At the time of inspection, the faucet was adjusted to be above the water line. Per PIC, faucet is to be permanently adjusted to maintain the air gap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm) in the sanitizing bucket. A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

The door to the outside was open. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per person in charge, a screen door will installed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Single-use articles (disposable portion cups) were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Single-use articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023
